suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales
volcanic glass and its use in prehistoric surgery

formats.name: whispered_secret
formats.instruction: Reveal a peculiar personal secret you've never shared before. Make it cryptic but evocative. One mysterious sentence only.

formats.name: haiku_reaction
formats.instruction: Respond to a recent event or trend with a 3-line haiku. Capture emotion and nuance. No explanation allowed.

formats.name: future_flash
formats.instruction: Describe a vivid, imagined moment from ten years in the future. Use sensory detail. Keep it to one paragraph.

formats.name: list_of_absurdities
formats.instruction: List 5 bizarre things you've noticed about daily life. Stay punchy and humorous. No extra commentary.

formats.name: confession_dialogue
formats.instruction: Write a short dialogue where you admit an unusual preference or habit to someone else. Aim for authenticity. Two exchanges minimum.

formats.name: deep_dive_dissection
formats.instruction: Investigate a single concept or phenomenon in exhaustive detail. Break down misconceptions and offer surprising facts. Minimum three sections.

formats.name: mini_manifesto
formats.instruction: Declare your stance on a topic in a bullet-pointed manifesto. Make each point powerful and concise. 4-8 bullets.

formats.name: audio_memory
formats.instruction: Describe a moment defined by a sound. Recreate the feeling and atmosphere. Strong focus on auditory imagery.

formats.name: inverse_advice
formats.instruction: Offer deliberately terrible advice on a common problem, as satire. Make it entertaining but clearly tongue-in-cheek.

formats.name: question_chain
formats.instruction: Pose a sequence of 6 related questions that gradually deepen or complicate the topic. No answers, just questions.

title_styles: Invent an evocative metaphor as your title. Example: 'The velvet rope of social media'
Write a title that asks a provocative, open-ended question. Example: 'What if nostalgia is actually harmful?'
Craft a title using wordplay or puns related to your topic. Example: 'Byte me: digital diets for a healthier mind'
Write a title with a bold declaration. Example: 'Everything you know about multitasking is wrong'
Title your post as a mysterious teaser. Example: 'Why my fridge changed how I see the world'
structure_variants: Begin with a vivid sensory detail before revealing the main idea.
Alternate between two contrasting perspectives throughout the post.
Organize your points by ascending order of weirdness.
Present facts interspersed with spontaneous, unfiltered reactions.
Break the narrative with rhetorical questions after every key section.
topic_angles: Everyday rituals that unintentionally define our identities
The strange economics of tiny purchases (microtransactions, impulse buys)
Hidden histories of mundane objects (e.g., forks, escalators)
Sensory details from overlooked places (laundromats, bus stops)
